headline: Survey at 22% target: strong regional concentration, clear expansion mandate emerging.
collection_status: 299 responses of 1,350 target (22.1% complete). Collection concentrated in 3 regions (LINDI, MARA, TANGA = 167 responses, 55.9%). Remaining 7 regions significantly under-represented; RUKWA and MANYARA critically low (5 and 4 responses).
key_findings: Expansion dominates stakeholder priorities: 110/299 (36.8%) prioritise expanding KiuFunza, vs. 56 (18.7%) for strengthening existing work—signals strong appetite for scale.

Teacher engagement solid but concentrated: 106 lower-primary KF teachers responding; head teachers well-represented (73), but only 2 non-KF teachers and 7 regional coordinators suggests limited external buy-in.
Partner satisfaction high (4.4/5) but scale readiness weak (2.7/5): stakeholders trust the programme but doubt capacity to expand without operational strengthening.
Feedback emphasises government integration and incentives: recurring themes are policy alignment, teacher motivation schemes, and digital monitoring—not just programme reach.
flags: Geographic imbalance critical: RUKWA and MANYARA severely under-sampled (9 responses combined, <3% of total). Risk survey findings skew toward high-performing regions and mask real implementation barriers.
Scale readiness-ambition gap: 36.8% want expansion but only 2.7/5 confidence in readiness. Likely indicates stakeholders see opportunity but fear operational collapse without support infrastructure.
Regional coordinator representation negligible: 7 RCs and 2 REOs among 299 responses. District-level buy-in may be weak; expansion plans risk failure without district ownership.
recommendation: Immediately intensify data collection in RUKWA, MANYARA, SINGIDA, and SHINYANGA (target 200+ additional responses within 6 weeks). Simultaneously conduct 5-8 rapid qualitative interviews with district and regional officials to diagnose the scale readiness gap and identify non-negotiable operational prerequisites before committing to expansion roadmap.
